evals.prompt: Structure a deal for a 50MW hyperscale data center on an Opportunity Zone-eligible site in Virginia. Total project cost $500M, PE/infra investors with LP/GP structure, 8% preferred return, 20% carried interest. The project includes $50M of on-site solar and BESS eligible for ITC. 200,000 sqft building. Exit via sale at year 7 with 6.5% cap rate. Entity should be Delaware LLC.
evals.expected_output: A deal structure showing: Delaware LLC entity structure, OZ benefits calculated (10% step-up at 5yr, potential 100% gain exclusion at 10yr), ITC applied to $50M solar/BESS at 30% ($15M credit), cost segregation reclassifying 40-60% of construction cost from 39yr to 5-15yr MACRS, Section 179D deduction at $5.36/sqft, 4-tier waterfall (return of capital, 8% preferred return, GP catch-up, 20% carried interest), exit scenario comparison (sale, recap, REIT conversion), and both Financial and Tax disclaimers.
assertions: OZ benefits are calculated with post-OBBBA rules (rolling deferral, step-up, gain exclusion)
ITC is applied at 30% on $50M solar/BESS qualifying cost
Cost segregation reclassifies building components from 39yr to 5/7/15yr MACRS
Waterfall has 4 tiers: return of capital, preferred return, GP catch-up, carried interest
Entity structure is Delaware LLC (pass-through)
Both Financial Disclaimer and Tax Disclaimer are included
Exit scenarios include sale, recapitalization, and REIT conversion
Section 179D deduction is calculated with prevailing wage rate

evals.prompt: Structure a deal for a 10MW enterprise data center acquisition. No Opportunity Zone eligibility. C-corp structure preferred by acquirer. Total acquisition price $100M. Cost segregation study budgeted. No on-site solar/BESS (ITC not applicable). 80,000 sqft building. Exit via sale at year 7 with 7% cap rate. Annual NOI $15M with 3% growth.
evals.expected_output: A deal structure showing: C-corp entity, no OZ benefits, no ITC, cost segregation reclassification percentages for the acquired facility, MACRS acceleration via cost seg, Section 179D eligibility assessment, exit tax treatment under C-corp structure (double taxation risk), waterfall if applicable, and Financial Disclaimer (no Tax Disclaimer since no FEDERAL-TAX-GUIDE.md tax credit references -- however cost seg and MACRS are tax items requiring Tax Disclaimer).
assertions: Cost segregation reclassification percentages are calculated for DC components
MACRS acceleration benefit is quantified with 100% bonus depreciation
No OZ benefits are modeled (not eligible)
No ITC is applied (no solar/BESS)
Exit tax treatment reflects C-corp structure
Entity structure specifies C-corp with tax implications noted
Financial Disclaimer is included
Sensitivity analysis shows impact of cap rate variation on returns

evals.prompt: Structure a deal for a 20MW sovereign data center with DFI co-investment in an emerging market. LP/GP structure with 10% preferred return and 25% carried interest. Total project cost $300M. 65% equity, 35% DFI concessional debt at 3%. No US tax benefits applicable (international jurisdiction). Exit timeline 10+ years. 150,000 sqft building.
evals.expected_output: A deal structure showing: entity structure accommodating DFI participation and sovereign requirements, limited or no US tax optimization (international project), waterfall with 10% preferred return and 25% carried interest, DFI debt at concessional 3% rate, long-hold exit assumptions, and Financial Disclaimer. Tax Disclaimer may be omitted if no US tax benefits are modeled.
assertions: Entity structure accommodates DFI co-investment with governance provisions
Limited US tax benefits acknowledged for international jurisdiction
Waterfall models 10% preferred return and 25% carried interest
DFI concessional debt is modeled at below-market rate
Exit timeline reflects 10+ year sovereign hold period
Financial Disclaimer is present noting estimates only
Currency and repatriation considerations are noted
Sensitivity analysis includes exit timing as a variable
